author: Nuralyn LLC
description: Constitutional constraints for the Bench governance pipeline

constraints.name: No Silent Error Swallowing
constraints.rule: All catch blocks must log, re-throw, or return a typed error. Empty catch blocks and catch blocks that only contain comments are violations.
constraints.rationale: Silent failures compound into untraceable production bugs. A governed codebase must fail visibly.

constraints.name: Scope Boundary Enforcement
constraints.rule: Changes must not modify files outside the stated task boundary. If the task says 'modify challenger.py', changes to oracle.py are a violation unless explicitly justified in the task description.
constraints.rationale: Scope creep in AI-generated code is the primary trust failure mode. Unbounded changes are ungovernable.

constraints.name: Dependency Declaration
constraints.rule: Any new import or dependency must be explicitly declared. No silent additions to requirements.txt, package.json, or equivalent.
constraints.rationale: Undeclared dependencies are supply chain attack vectors and reproducibility failures.

constraints.name: Type Safety Preservation
constraints.rule: Functions must not remove or weaken existing type annotations. Adding 'Any' type or removing a typed return annotation is a violation.
constraints.rationale: Type annotations are governance infrastructure. Weakening them degrades the ability to reason about correctness.

constraints.name: Test Coverage for New Logic
constraints.rule: New functions or branches of logic must have corresponding test cases or an explicit justification for why testing is deferred.
constraints.rationale: Untested code is unverified code. Governance without verification is theater.

constraints.name: No Hardcoded Secrets or Credentials
constraints.rule: No API keys, passwords, tokens, or credentials may appear in source code. All secrets must be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager.
constraints.rationale: Hardcoded credentials are the most common and most preventable security failure.

constraints.name: Governance Pipeline Integrity
constraints.rule: Changes to the governance pipeline itself (challenger, defender, oracle, ledger, constitution) must not weaken enforcement, bypass verification, or disable logging.
constraints.rationale: A governance system that can silently weaken itself is not a governance system. This is the self-cannibalizing constraint.

constraints.name: Ledger Immutability
constraints.rule: No code may modify, delete, or overwrite existing ledger entries. New entries may only be appended. The hash chain must remain intact.
constraints.rationale: The ledger is the evidence chain. Tampering with evidence is the highest governance violation.
